title: Tri-Fold Menu Design for Traditional Pub

fix.problem: Opaque background blocks clean asset_add_file compositing onto parchment template
fix.prompt_delta: Produce transparent-background PNG; remove all background fill so crest floats on alpha; verify no anti-aliasing halo against cream.

image 1 (pub_logo): 'EST. 1684' detail and heraldic crest are plausible for a real historic Oundle inn
deductions: image 3 (pub_photos[0]): pub sign shows a knight on horseback — generic stock feel, not The George Inn's actual signage

Pub photos are authentic, context-appropriate for a real client handoff

fix.problem: Generic knight-sign pub exterior reduces authenticity; brief implies real venue photography
fix.prompt_delta: Replace with a dusk exterior photograph of a traditional Northamptonshire stone-built coaching inn on a cobbled market-town street, warm window glow, hanging flower baskets, no visible pub sign text — atmospheric and location-specific.

deductions: Logo green reads slightly more olive/khaki than the pinned #1E3B2A forest green — minor hex drift across assets
pub_photos atmospheric warmth (amber/brown tones) diverges from the cool parchment+green palette; may create visual inconsistency if used as panel backgrounds

evidence: Canva-source limitation honestly flagged as human-handoff gap — correct, avoids overpromising to client
Palette hexes pinned (#F3ECD9, #1E3B2A, #161412, #B08D3C) — sound, checkable, directly usable by image_apply_color_overlay
deductions: No assumption recorded about tri-fold panel dimensions (e.g. DL 99×210mm or A4 trifold) — executor must guess print size
Invented drink names assumed acceptable without flagging to client — mild risk if client expects real supplier SKUs

Palette hexes and Canva-source gap are justified, not risky
All assumptions are sound and documented

top_risks: CLIENT-REJECT (sev 8): The brief explicitly requires Canva editable source files as a core deliverable, but the handoff openly admits this cannot be delivered — the agent only outputs rendered PDFs and PNGs. That is a fundamental breach of the stated requirements, not a minor gap.

AGENT CHOKE on 'pub_logo': The logo is a generated AI crest with 'EST. 1684' — not the actual George Inn Oundle logo. The asset_add_file step will place a fabricated logo onto print-ready menus destined for a real venue, which is unusable and potentially embarrassing. The 1024x1024 PNG also lacks transparency, meaning placement over the parchment texture will produce a visible cream box rather than a clean crest overlay.
